nodes.context: American Mafia family based in New York City, originally the Profaci family.
Original name of the Colombo crime family under Joe Profaci.
American Mafia family, formed after Albert Anastasia's murder.
New England Mafia family whose boss Raymond L.S. Patriarca negotiated a peace agreement.
American Mafia family; a soldier participated in Gallo's murder.
New York City Mafia family, originally the Profaci family, involved in organized crime.
New York City Mafia family, one of the Five Families.
New York City Mafia family, one of the Five Families.
New York City Mafia family, one of the Five Families.
Crew within the Colombo/Profaci family led by Joe Gallo and his brothers.

edges.evidence_quote: the Colombo crime family of New York City ... Gallo became an enforcer in the Profaci crime family
edges.context: The Colombo family is the successor to the Profaci family.

edges.evidence_quote: Joe Profaci allegedly asked the Gallo crew to murder Albert Anastasia, the boss of what was to become the Gambino crime family
edges.context: Profaci conspired with Gambino to murder Anastasia.

edges.evidence_quote: Patriarca family boss Raymond L.S. Patriarca negotiated a peace agreement between the two factions
edges.context: Patriarca mediated a peace between Colombo factions.
A Genovese soldier participated in the murder of Joe Gallo, acting with Colombo associates.

edges.evidence_quote: Umbertos was owned by associates of the Genovese crime family, which would normally imply the Genoveses had given their blessing to a killing on their territory, but Luparelli's account, that the shooting was a spontaneous unplanned act without approval from high-ranking mafiosi, took pressure off the feuding Colombo and Genovese families.
edges.context: The Colombo and Genovese families were feuding, but the account of the Gallo murder reduced tensions between them.

edges.evidence_quote: Albert and his remaining crew to join the Genovese family.
edges.context: After a 1974 agreement, Albert Gallo and his remaining crew joined the Genovese family.

edges.evidence_quote: Bobby Boriello – transferred to Gambino crime family in 1972
edges.context: Gallo crew member Bobby Boriello transferred to the Gambino crime family in 1972.

edges.evidence_quote: Vic Amuso – transferred to Lucchese crime family
edges.context: Gallo crew member Vic Amuso transferred to the Lucchese crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: Nicholas Bianco – transferred to Patriarca crime family in 1963
edges.context: Gallo crew member Nicholas Bianco transferred to the Patriarca crime family in 1963.

edges.evidence_quote: Michael Rizzitello – transferred to Los Angeles crime family
edges.context: Gallo crew member Michael Rizzitello transferred to the Los Angeles crime family.
